steps.title: Spot the move from general to specific
steps.content: Sentence 2 introduces a broad claim: some of the 206 parades honored aviation. Sentence 3 then specifies: Corrigan's parade was one of 16 honoring pilots. The transition must signal this shift from general statement to particular confirmation.

steps.title: Match the transition that marks confirmation
steps.content:
”Indeed” works here because it reinforces or confirms what was just claimed generally — it signals that what follows validates the general point. “In addition” would indicate separate information, “Therefore” would wrongly imply consequence, and “Nevertheless” would wrongly introduce contrast.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
